question_text: Let {𝑋𝑛}𝑛≥1 be a sequence of random variables having the following probability mass function 𝑃(𝑋𝑛= 𝑥) = 1 5𝑛(1 −1 5𝑛) , 𝑥= 0, 1, 2, … ; 𝑛∈ℕ.

options.D: efine 𝑍𝑛= 𝑛 , 𝑛∈ℕ, and let 𝑉 be a random variable. If 𝑍𝑛 𝑑→ 𝑉, as 𝑛→∞, then which of the following statements is/are correct?
options.A: 𝑉 has normal distribution with mean 5 and variance 25
options.B: 𝑉 has chi-square distribution with 5 degrees of freedom
options.C: 𝑉 has exponential distribution with mean 5
